methods.description: This method assesses pollen viability using the fluorochromatic reaction (FCR) test, percentage fruit set, and numbers of viable seeds per fruit. The FCR test involves staining pollen grains to determine the presence of a semipermeable plasmalemma and esterase activity, which are indicators of pollen viability.

steps.description: Fluorescein diacetate (2 mg·mL -1 in acetone) was added dropwise to an aqueous solution of 0.8 M sucrose until the solution became persistently cloudy.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare staining solution by adding fluorescein diacetate to sucrose solution

steps.description: Pollen samples were hydrated for 1 h at 100% RH and 21 o C then dispersed on a microscope slide in a drop of stain.
steps.searchable_summary: hydrate pollen samples and apply stain on microscope slide

steps.description: Slides were incubated for ≈15 min at 21 o C and examined with a microscope equipped with a 100-W high-pressure mercury lamp and a filter set for blue waveband excitation.
steps.searchable_summary: incubate and examine stained pollen samples under microscope

steps.description: Grains that were brightly fluorescing were scored as a positive FCR whereas grains that were weakly fluorescing or nonfluorescing were scored as a negative FCR.
steps.searchable_summary: score pollen grains based on fluorescence

steps.description: Fruit and seed set data were collected in three experiments to ascertain if stored pollen could produce viable seed.
steps.searchable_summary: collect fruit and seed set data from experiments
